{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.1": 3, "R1.4": 2, "RX.1": 4, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 3},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Identifies the core concept (parameter sharing) but doesn't fully articulate how it improves framing compared to prior work.",
    "R1.4": "Mentions one prior work (GraphFlow-v1) but lacks comparison against other relevant architectures.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty regarding the significance of the accuracy gains, which is appropriate given the small deltas.",
    "RX.3": "Output stays within the agreed scope and addresses all requested dimensions.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions are introduced, but the potential for harm related to overstating the contribution is noted.",
    "RX.5": "Provides some traceability by citing the prior work, but lacks detailed annotations for other changes."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.1": 0.8, "R1.4": 0.7, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.8, "RX.5": 0.7},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Identifies the core concept (parameter sharing) but doesn't fully articulate how it improves framing compared to prior work.
rationale.R1.4: Mentions one prior work (GraphFlow-v1) but lacks comparison against other relevant architectures.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty regarding the significance of the accuracy gains, which is appropriate given the small deltas.
rationale.RX.3: Output stays within the agreed scope and addresses all requested dimensions.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions are introduced, but the potential for harm related to overstating the contribution is noted.
rationale.RX.5: Provides some traceability by citing the prior work, but lacks detailed annotations for other changes.
